mechanism_narrative: A falling federal funds rate lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is in a downward trend.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often than negative when FEDFUNDS is declining on a 3-month basis, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An inflation spike reduces real rate visibility and forces higher expected policy rates, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days after CPI inflation accelerates sharply.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.4% month-over-month or the 3-month annualized CPI trend exceeds the prior 6-month trend by 1.0pp
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show negative median 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns occur in more than 60% of inflation-spike triggers.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening household income and slower sales growth, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are marked down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not negative on average after unemployment rises 0.3pp in 3 months, or if the sign is positive in a majority of such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll growth and unemployment improvement occur together, the labor market is confirming broad demand strength, supporting revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average rises and UNRATE falls over the same 3-month window
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-down condition does not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 20-day returns, or if hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in retail sales indicates stronger consumer demand and near-term earnings momentum, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positively associated with 3-month RSAFS growth in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling from already weak levels signals tightening credit conditions and slower cyclically sensitive demand, which should generate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 8% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST declines are not followed by below-median 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal flips positive in repeated downturn episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production and payrolls improve together, firms are expanding output with labor support, which signals stronger real activity and should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint increase in INDPRO and PAYEMS does not improve 5-day return hit rate versus either proxy alone, or if the effect disappears in rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a period of elevated policy rates, weak GDP growth combined with soft retail sales reflects stalled aggregate demand and tighter real financing conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% and GDPC1 annualized growth is below 2.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the combined high-rate/low-growth/weak-sales regime does not precede negative 60-day SPX returns, or if positive returns dominate in triggered episodes.
